response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense-funded ARPANET, demonstrated in 1969, and grew through the 1970s, leading to the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP that laid its modern foundation. Key milestones include Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (with HTML, URLs, HTTP) and 1993’s Mosaic browser, which made the web accessible; the 1990s dot-com boom (followed by a 2001 crash, with survivors like Amazon and Google); the 2000s rise of social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube); and 2007’s iPhone-driven mobile internet and 2006’s AWS-led cloud computing. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly all aspects of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
